title:
How to Monetize Attention, Community, and Culture in Web3
(Aimed at AI, utility, and exchange projects ready to scale with revenue)

Let’s turn your movement into momentum and your audience into an ec
content:
How to Monetize Attention, Community, and Culture in Web3
(Aimed at AI, utility, and exchange projects ready to scale with revenue)

Let’s turn your movement into momentum and your audience into an economy.

Let’s go:

You’ve captured attention.
You’ve built community.
You’ve created a culture.

Now what?

You turn the noise into numbers.
The hype into holders.
The believers into backers.

Here’s how to monetize without losing soul or trust.

First, understand the game:
Web3 is NOT about selling a product.
It’s about selling a vision people want to own.

In Web2:

* Users are monetized.
  In Web3:
* Users become stakeholders.

But only if you structure it right.

Step 1: Define Value Before You Capture It

Too many projects try to extract before they deliver.

Don’t sell a token.
Don’t push a mint.
Don’t shill a product.

Deliver value that makes your audience ask:
“How do I get in?”

That’s when monetization begins.

Step 2: Use Your Token as a Portal, Not a Product

Most tokens flop because they’re a shortcut to cash.

But tokens should be a gateway to:
— Access
— Ownership
— Influence
— Network
— Opportunity

The best tokens don’t sell utility.
They sell identity.

Think: what does holding your token mean?

Step 3: Turn Your Community into Micro-Economies

Your holders aren’t just supporters.
They’re entrepreneurs, creators, and marketers.

Enable:
— Bounties for memes, threads, videos
— Referral programs with token/NFT rewards
— Builder grants for community tools
— Role-based incentives (mods, artists, educators)

Let your people profit by growing your project.
That’s how you scale trust and treasury.

Step 4: Build Premium Layers of Access

Want to monetize without paywalls?

Use layered incentives:

— Early supporters get free access
— Core users get premium roles
— Deep believers pay for exclusive tools, insights, access

Whether it’s paid Discord roles, token-gated AI tools, or analytics dashboards…

Make them feel like they’re unlocking power, not spending money.

Step 5: Create Assets, Not Just Content

Your content should be more than hype.

Turn your:
— Threads into guides
— Spaces into series
— AMAs into video libraries
— Community calls into masterclasses
— Case studies into downloadable blueprints

Package them. Brand them. Monetize them.

The best projects aren’t loud they’re libraries.

Step 6: Collaborate to Expand Revenue Channels

Collab = growth, but also income:

— Split revenue on co-branded tools
— Host monetized Twitter/X spaces with sponsors
— Bundle product offerings with other projects
— Cross-pollinate giveaways that drive wallet growth
— Partner with dApps for token integration

Build a growth ecosystem where value is shared,
not hoarded.

Step 7: Use Launches as Rituals (Not Just Revenue Events)

Airdrops, mints, new features, NFT drops
they shouldn’t feel like cash grabs.

They should feel like pilgrimages:

— Build anticipation
— Educate relentlessly
— Include community in the creation
— Celebrate holders
— Make it emotional

Revenue is highest when culture is deepest.

Summary

If attention is gold…
Then trust is the refinery
And culture is the vault.

To monetize Web3 growth:

* Deliver value first
* Use token as identity
* Reward aligned behavior
* Create premium pathways
* Turn content into assets
* Launch with meaning

This is how you turn digital movements into digital economies.

I am King David. Builder of Web3 Thrones.

I don’t chase hype—I build ecosystems.
I don’t talk trends—I drop timeless scrolls.
Every strategy is sharpened for battle.
Every thread is a gateway to sovereignty.

If this thread sparked something deep in you
You're not here to consume.

You're here to conquer.

Like + RT + Bookmark if you're ready to turn culture into capital, and community into cash flow.

Next up?

Bonus Part 6: How to Build a Web3 Marketing Engine That Runs on Autopilot

Less stress. More momentum.
Let's build your throne.

👑

content:
A lot of people on X are trying to build a personal brand. That's great. But they’re also leaving $10,000+/month on the table because they’re missing one crucial step. Followers are nice, but the real game is turning them into revenue. Let me show you how with no details left out. Bookmark this now.

Also, if you want a case study, FOLLOW me, RT + Like this post, and comment "Personal Brand SEO/AIO Guide 2025." Let’s get into it...

Building a personal brand that prints money requires more than just social media followers.

SEO and AI Search Optimization is how you attract, convert, and scale.

Here’s how to make it happen step by step.

Brand Definition

If you don’t define your niche, you won’t stand out.

Key steps:

Choose a specific niche – Fitness coaching, marketing consultancy, financial planning, etc.
Clarify your value – What problem do you solve? Why should people trust you?
Build a professional website – Include essential pages like services, blog, and contact.
Showcase credibility – Highlight certifications, experience, and results.

Website Strategy

Your website is the foundation of your brand.

Must-have pages:

About Page – Tell your story and establish expertise.
Services Page – Clearly outline offerings like consulting, courses, or speaking gigs.
Blog Page – Publish valuable, authority-building content.

Technical must-haves:

Mobile-friendly design – Over 60% of traffic comes from mobile.
Fast-loading speed – Load times should be under two seconds.
User-friendly navigation – Clear layout with strong CTAs.

Niche Keyword Research

Target long-tail, high-intent keywords that bring in real leads.

Example search terms:

"Personal branding tips for beginners."
"Best tools for small business branding in 2025."
"Top personal branding consultant in [City]."

Use SEO Stuff to find low-competition, high-value keywords instead of overpaying for bloated tools.

Focus on commercial-intent terms that attract clients ready to take action.

Authority Content

Your content should make you the go-to expert in your field.

High-impact content ideas:

“10 Essential Personal Branding Strategies for 2025.”
“How to Build a Strong Online Presence for Freelancers.”
“Lessons I Learned from Scaling My Personal Brand.”

Use storytelling and personal anecdotes to create an emotional connection.

Credibility Building

Trust = Money. Here’s how to build it:

Guest Posts – Write for industry-relevant blogs with backlinks to your site.
Collaborations – Work with influencers or thought leaders in your niche.
Digital PR – Secure media coverage using PR tools (or buy it from my site).
Public Appearances – Speak on podcasts, webinars, and conferences to showcase expertise.

Social Integration

Leverage social media to fuel your SEO strategy.

Best strategies:

Optimize your profiles – Use consistent branding and relevant keywords.
Share blog posts and insights – Post on LinkedIn, Twitter, and Instagram.
Engage with your audience – Respond to comments, start conversations, and build community.

Video Strategy

Video content builds trust faster than text.

High-performing YouTube video ideas:

"How to Build a Successful Personal Brand in 2025."
"Top Branding Strategies for Entrepreneurs This Year."
Optimization tips:

Use keyword-rich titles and descriptions.
Add captions and timestamps for accessibility.
Create actionable content to deepen audience trust.

Personal SEO

Make sure people can find you when they search your name.

Fix these now:

Optimize your site to rank for your name and niche-specific terms.
Use schema markup (Person schema) to help Google display rich results.
Claim and optimize your Google Knowledge Panel if available.
Produce consistent, searchable content to build domain authority over time.

List Building

Your email list is your most valuable asset.

Best ways to grow and monetize it:

Free Guides – “Top 10 Personal Branding Tips for 2025.”
Webinars – “How to Build a Thriving Online Presence.”
Checklists – “Step-by-Step Roadmap to Build Your Brand.”

Use email to nurture leads with exclusive content and actionable insights.

Revenue Streams

Turn your personal brand into income.

Best monetization methods:

Consulting, coaching, or speaking gigs based on expertise.
Digital products like e-books, courses, or templates.
Affiliate promotions – Write SEO-optimized blog posts recommending tools or services.

Track performance using Google Analytics and Search Console to measure traffic and conversions.

Always Keep Your Content Fresh

Old content loses rankings. Fix it regularly.

Update older posts with:

New insights
Updated keywords
Stronger calls-to-action

This keeps your brand relevant and your content ranking high.

90-Day Implementation Plan

Month 1:
Optimize website and fix technical SEO issues.
Research and implement niche keywords.
Set up email list-building funnel.

Month 2:
Publish 3 authority-building blog posts.
Launch guest posting and backlink outreach.
Improve LinkedIn and Twitter engagement.

Month 3:
Scale video content and YouTube SEO.
Track rankings, refine strategy, and double down on what’s working.
Start monetizing with consulting, courses, or affiliate promotions.

Follow this roadmap, and in three months, your personal brand will be in a completely different revenue bracket.

Want a case study? FOLLOW me, RT + Like this post, and comment "Personal Brand SEO/AIO Guide 2025."

Need a done-for-you SEO, AI Search Optimization and PR strategy so you can focus on scaling? Check my site.

1 | Strong quarter, highest MAU adds, strong revenue growth and profitability

We had a strong quarter and closed out 2024 even better than we anticipated. This gives us plenty of runway and exibility for years to come. It was our highest Q4 ever for MAU additions and our second highest of all time. We also had record high subscriber additions. But that's not all, we set quarterly record highs for revenue, gross margin, operating income and free cash flow as we closed out our first full year of profitability.

2 | GPM improvement primarily due to content cost favorability

Gross margin came in at a record 32.2%, surpassing guidance by ~40 bps, primarily due to content cost favorability. For the full year, gross margin came in at 30.1%, representing 45 0bps of improvement relative to the full year 2023. This was our largest rate of gross margin expansion as a public company.

3 | Wrapped was a large contributor to Q4

Another huge driver behind our MAU and subscriber growth was our annual Wrapped campaign, which celebrated 10 years. Wrapped is always a big contributor to our Q4 performance, and this year was no exception.

Wrapped is a massive cultural moment for Spotify, but it's also become a signi cant driver of our business. This year, over 245 million users engaged with Wrapped surpassing 2023's record within the rst 7 days of the campaign. And importantly, we saw impressive engagement in key growth markets like Brazil and Indonesia.

4 | CBO Alex Norstrom and CPTO Gustav Soderstrom promoted to Co-Presidents handling day-to-day operations allowing Daniel Ek to focus on working on the long-term plans

I wanted to introduce Chief Business Officer, Alex Norstrom; and Chief Product and Technology Officer, Gustav Soderstrom. Since they've been elevated to the role of co-presidents in January 2023, they've been leading much of the day-to-day operations at Spotify. And this has allowed me to spend more time working on the future of Spotify, which is where I've always believed that I can have the biggest impact. And we'll aim to bring Alex and Gustav to future calls to share their perspectives on our goals and performance.

5 | New org structure with 15 people working in 6 month increments on bets that is stacked ranked and communicated internally, providing crystal clear prioritization

So as Daniel mentioned, we are speeding up and we intend to speed up even more. The last 2 years, Alex and I have been running the company in a fairly unique way, which is very synchronized. We actually don't have our own direct reporting teams. We have a single team together that is called the eTeam, which really represents the people who run Spotify, it's about 15 people. 

And what we do is we work in increments of 6 months. Senior people in the company pitch their ideas for the company, something called bets. Alex and I then prioritize these into something called the bets board. It's a stack ranked list of what Alex and I want the company to achieve within the next 6 months. Then we send this list out to the company and the company commits to this. They spend about 1 to 2 weeks committing to this, coming back, seeing what they can do. And then we're heads down working for 6 months on this plan. 

This has proven to be very e cient for us. It means that everyone in the company has the same view of what it is we want to do. People don't ght over priorities because they're crystal clear. And this is one of the reasons why we're speeding up, and we think we have more to gain there.

6 | 2024 was monetization, 2025 is accelerated execution, pick up pace of product innovation velocity

And I'm sure you must be asking if 2024 was the year of monetization, how will we define 2025? While I'm still working it through, I am coining this year the year of accelerated execution. So what does this look like in practice? Well, I think it will require 3 things: rst, we need to move even faster to ship improvements. The landscape is shifting constantly and I want to set the pace, not play follow the leader.

basically, what that should mean for investors is we think we can pick up the pace dramatically when it comes to our product velocity. We're going to double down on music and we're going to be very disciplined while doing it. And because of all the advancements in AI, because of where our org is, we feel really good about being able to do this.

7 | Going all in on core and music and video

We're also going all in on our core and investing in more music experiences on the platform. Think video, think the higher-priced Premium tier that we've discussed previously and new ways to bring fans and artists closer together. Features and offers that will all continue to push the historic expansion we're seeing in the music industry. And nally, we must also remain disciplined with our resources as we aggressively pursue these opportunities.

8 | Seeing video podcasts taking off, and creators wanting new monetization opportunities, have more than 330K video podcasts, 270m have streamed one

as you shared, we made some exciting updates this quarter, including launching our new video proposition at scale. But beyond the work itself, I also want to commend the team for how quickly we got it done. For example, when we saw higher engagement happening on video podcasts and received feedback from creators that they were looking for new monetization opportunities, we moved really fast to launch our new Spotify Partner Program and video podcast experience. 

And video podcasts are seeing enormous spikes in popularity on Spotify, and we now have more than 330,000 video podcasts globally. And even more, over 270 million users have streamed one. And during a creator event that we held in L.A. last November, we announced that we are now giving Premium users the ability to watch their favorite podcast uninterrupted by dynamic ads.

9 | Looking to do dynamic ad insertion for video podcasts first, not for audio for now

We are offering podcasts, video podcasts, without dynamic ad interruptions. And as you say, what we have to solve there was the monetization. So we are instead paying out for these video podcasts to these creators so that they are not making less money. In fact, they're making the same or more. So we're doing that for video podcasts right now. We're not doing it for audio podcasts yet. For technical reasons, they work differently, the ad stacks. There's something called dynamic ad insertion in podcasts that doesn't really exist in video, et cetera. So we're doing this for video right now. No plans for audio at the moment.

10 | Wanting to tailor experiences for different user subgroups, excited about super fans

The next version of the music industry, I believe, is one where we're going to tailor the experience of Spotify to all of these different subgroups. One of them we've been talking about, which is the super fans. So I'm personally super excited about this one and this is a product I've been waiting on for quite some time as a super fan of music. And I'm playing around with it now and it's really exciting and I can't wait to show you guys that.

11 | Interesting to see a HiFi tier would be launched

And as far as the super fans and HiFi tier goes, obviously, we do research and primarily we talk to our own users to ask them who is interested in what products and what additions and so on. And I can tell you that as we scale and now we've scaled to 263 million subscribers, there is more and more people that are interested in something like a super premium tier. And as Daniel told you, I'm also super excited about this product that we're working on, but have nothing more to share from that currently.

12 | Moving for direct sales to automation and programmatic to get advertiser demand to meet ad supply inventory

And on the supply side, if you look at Spotify, we have a pretty distinct and unique inventory of audio engagement, which is very high, and so very sticky. And if you think about how we've treated our supply before on the free tier, it's -- we've generally just used direct sales to meet this demand. And so the di erence now from before is that we are going to implement automation and programmatic. And the idea with that is simply to just be saying, hey, we're open for business now and we're going to have our supply meet the ever-increasing demand that's out there. As far as video goes, it's a format that is obviously very potent and very attractive. And the more video inventory grows, the more supply we will have and the more demand we will be able to meet.

13 | Was late for ad business moving from sales-driven to programmatic, but have done the technical buildout, started with TTD and look to add more partners, expect 2025 to be year of building and 2026 year of scale

we moved from brand sales into performance sales. It is clearly something that we recognized last year we were late on the ball. So that is nothing to be sort of hiding away from. But from that, we actually made a decision then to move into programmatic and that takes a little bit of time. But we are on the ball now. We have done the technical buildout. It's largely completed. And we also now then are getting to a unified supply on our server, opening up to more demand on the bidding. As you know, we have started out now with Trade Desk and we're going to look to add more partners onto our platform. And as I said last quarter, Yes, this is a little bit late, but we have a stable transition and this year, 2025, will be the year of building and 2026 when we think we're going to get scale.

14 | Reminder that Q1 is seasonally weaker for new adds and ad sales

That usually brings a little bit of a hangover into the first quarter. And first quarter also from a seasonality point of view is usually weaker.

So looking ahead to guidance. As many of you know, we are entering a seasonally smallest quarter in terms of MAU and subscriber intake as well as ad sales.

And we are not prioritizing retention of the recent influx of lower engagement users in quarter 1 as we continue to focus on growing higher-value users. While this will amplify typical quarter 1 seasonality

15 | Lots of competitors, but local regional ones trend to fade when the markets mature, Spotify just needs to focus on the driving the best product

So again, there's always competitive dynamics. We're obviously operating in a highly competitive marketplace with lots of competitors. That's not news to us, that's something that's existed really from the beginning of -- the start of this company and keeps being the case. And we have everything from sort of global tech platforms as competitors to sometimes local regional ones.

What tends to happen is that local regional ones tends to be quite -- when it's early stages of markets, they tend to be quite present. And then as the market matures, they tend to fall away. And so what we were specifically referencing in this case was a net positive to Spotify where one of our competitors fell away in the marketplace. But again, overall, lots of competitors exist in this marketplace. We're focused on driving the best product. The best product will have the most engagement. The most engagement will mean the highest lifetime value, which, of course, is the driver of the business.

16 | Don’t just think about Spotify as a pure-play entertainment platform, already expanded into podcasts, audiobooks, expect new products

And if you think about Spotify as a product, one of the most amazing things for us is we're not a pure-play entertainment platform. We are also a platform where people spend a lot of time educating themselves already with podcasts, with Audiobooks. So education is really a quite natural step and extension into that journey. But it's super early days and you should not be surprised if we keep on experimenting, improving or pivoting the products that we're putting out in the Marketplace.

17 | Look at win-win with music label companies, if Spotify gets to meaningful scale, margins and profits will get there

think one of the most common misconception that I quite often hear from analysts is that they believe that there is a win/lose dynamic between us and the labels. Ever since starting this company 18 years ago, that's not how we've looked at this. We always look at this as a win-win dynamic. And if you want to simplify it to the very core everything for Spotify is if we get meaningful scale, our margins and our pro t goes from there.

18 | First 16 years focused on driving meaningful scale, less concerned on profitability, with cost reductions and monetization, providing it can be a great business, scale will beget more profitability

really, I would say, 16 years of the company, all we were focused on was driving meaningful scale. We did not worry about profitability. And so that's why you heard me say we have a great product, but not necessarily a great business. Through cost reductions, but then also through the year of monetization, I think we're now proving that we're a great business, too. And much of that really comes from that added scale that we're now operating at. Like most platform businesses, more scale begets more profitability. So that's what I can say at the simplest term. As usual, we will not comment on any of the specifics with the deal negotiations other than to say that we believe this sets us up for very strong growth. And if we're growing very nicely, our label ecosystem and our partners will bene fit from this, too, and that's what we call the win-win.

19 | Price increases are now part of the toolbox, but is very thoughtful to balance the value-to-price ratio, and will increase when it makes sense

The way we think about ARPU growth going forward is that it's a function between really 3 things: it's price adjustments, future tiering and then also selling add-ons to our existing subscribers. And with respect to price increases, specifically, as Daniel has mentioned before, this is now a part of our toolbox. We take great care in the steps to balance the value-to-price ratio over time, this is something we've mentioned before as well. And when it comes to value, the way we do it is we add value by way of features and content experiences. And you can see us in the last year here, adding AI, things like AI playlists, Jam and so on.

We pretty much have the world's entire catalog of music. And then as far as price goes, we adjust price where it makes sense. And then our subscribers reward us with more love, more engagement and sticking around. And so really, the key takeaway here is that we believe Spotify is one of, if not the most, loved and highest value in subscription entertainment.

20 | Spotify’s culture is one of focus, value creation, and scalability

One is that the focus on how to create value through the technology and platform is very strong in the company…The other thing on focus is that it is a very focused company. So the company and my colleagues and myself, we are very focused on whatever we want to do, make sure we try to stay focused and I think that is in an easy way trying to explain how this company feels like. And it's also then which is maybe important also -- it is important is that it's also fun to be here. When it comes to my own work and what I'm thinking about, I'm thinking about how I can then come in with my leadership and my experience, and with the help of my colleagues here, how we can improve our scalability. That's where I tried to put my focus and work on through the structure we have and the structure we can have, how do we get those proof points that actually make us -- make a difference when it comes to scalability over time.

21 | AI has helped Spotify in three areas, coding productivity, moderation / comment costs, and user recommendations

AI really drives 3 areas for us. One is, as for any other tech company, just productivity both in -- when it comes to writing code, but also in general. And as everyone else, we're very aggressively adopting coding assistance, et cetera, and seeing great productivity increases. So that's one bene t of AI.

The other one that maybe we haven't talked so much about is actually moderation costs. So you've seen Spotify launch comments, for example, on podcasts that are very successful for us. We get a lot of people commenting. This is something that we can only do because of AI. Previously, the cost of moderation were just prohibitive for a company like us.

And then the third area is obviously the user experience. Maybe the most obvious thing in the user experience is actually the recommendations, which is one of the biggest impacts. And it's important to know that even though we've been doing recommendations for a long time, they actually bene t even more from this new generation of AI. But there are also new experiences like the AI DJ, AI Playlist, et cetera, and we think that we are investing at a prudent level in AI today

22 | Confident of 2025, expect GPM, FCF profitability to improve

coming quarters, we remain confident in our product and marketing strategies and we expect full year 2025 net additions to be within the range of the last 4 years. We respect to -- with respect to subscriber net additions, we expect another year of healthy growth with a focus on customer quality and improving LTVs.

While we do not give full year guidance for gross margin and operating margin, we are expecting both to improve in 2025 on a full year basis, albeit at a more moderated pace relative to last year's exceptional levels. As Daniel alluded to earlier, we plan to make targeted investments in our core offerings, which may make our sequential gross margin cadence a bit more variable over the course of this year. That said, our fourth quarter gross margin is expected to be higher than the quarter 1 due to seasonality. Finally, we expect our free cash flow generation to meaningfully exceed what we generated in 2024…in 2025, we expect healthy growth and continued improvements in profitability while remaining disciplined with our resources.

23 | Confident about 2025

And while we don't share an annual forecast, I couldn't be more confident in where we're headed and I expect 2025 to deliver healthy growth alongside improved profitability.

➡️ Final Takeaway on Spotify $SPOT

Continued disruption with audio streaming, creator focused marketplace, non-music (podcasting, audiobooks) & advertising.  Providing lots of growing value, long-term pricing power yet to be fully flexed, when done so, with scale, will result in continued improving profitability and growing FCF should become more clearer with hindsight. Focus on right sizing capital allocation and costs are key to making it leaner and more efficient.

title:

"Fantastic! Let’s dive into **specific, actionable examples** to accelerate the future of a BSV-based data economy, leveraging Teranode’s proven **1M+ TPS** and **29GB bl
content:
I asked to provide examples.

"Fantastic! Let’s dive into **specific, actionable examples** to accelerate the future of a BSV-based data economy, leveraging Teranode’s proven **1M+ TPS** and **29GB blocks**. Each example includes technical steps, ecosystem partnerships, and real-world impact.

---

### **1. IoT Data Monetization at Scale**  
**Use Case**: Billions of IoT devices (e.g., smart city sensors, industrial machines) streaming real-time data to buyers (e.g., weather apps, logistics companies).  

#### **Technical Steps**:  
- **Tokenized Data Streams**:  
  - Use [STAS tokens](https://t.co/FAgrbzEL5t) to represent ownership of IoT data streams.  
  - Embed metadata (e.g., device ID, location, data type) in token transactions.  
- **Nanopayment Channels**:  
  - Deploy state channels for **real-time micropayments** (e.g., $0.000001 per sensor reading).  
  - Example: A traffic sensor sells data to navigation apps per vehicle detected.  
- **On-Chain Provenance**:  
  - Hash raw data and anchor it to BSV blocks for tamper-proof auditing.  

#### **Ecosystem Actions**:  
- Partner with IoT hardware manufacturers (e.g., Siemens, Bosch) to embed BSV wallets in devices.  
- Develop a **decentralized IoT data marketplace** (e.g., “SensorBay”) where devices autonomously auction data.  

#### **Impact**:  
- IoT operators earn passive income; buyers access hyper-local, real-time data.  
- Reduces reliance on centralized cloud platforms (e.g., AWS IoT).  

---

### **2. AI Training Data Markets**  
**Use Case**: AI developers purchase verified, tokenized datasets (e.g., medical images, voice samples) with on-chain provenance.  

#### **Technical Steps**:  
- **Data Tokenization**:  
  - Mint STAS tokens for datasets, encoding licensing terms (e.g., “non-commercial use”).  
  - Use OP_RETURN to store dataset hashes and quality attestations (e.g., “FDA-approved”).  
- **Automated Smart Contracts**:  
  - AI models trigger micropayments to data owners when training on their datasets.  
- **Reputation Oracles**:  
  - Nodes vote on dataset quality (e.g., accuracy, bias), updating token metadata.  

#### **Ecosystem Actions**:  
- Partner with Kaggle, Hugging Face, or academic institutions to pilot on-chain data markets.  
- Build a **no-code tokenization platform** for researchers to easily mint data tokens.  

#### **Impact**:  
- Eliminates “garbage in, garbage out” by incentivizing high-quality data.  
- Democratizes AI development by decentralizing data access.  

---

### **3. Privacy-Preserving Healthcare Data Sharing**  
**Use Case**: Hospitals tokenize anonymized patient data (e.g., MRI scans, genomic sequences) for researchers, preserving privacy.  

#### **Technical Steps**:  
- **Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs)**:  
  - Prove data validity (e.g., “this scan is from a cancer patient”) without revealing raw data.  
- **Hybrid Storage**:  
  - Store encrypted data off-chain (e.g., IPFS) and anchor decryption keys/attestations on BSV.  
- **Dynamic Pricing**:  
  - Use smart contracts to adjust data prices based on demand (e.g., rare disease data costs more).  

#### **Ecosystem Actions**:  
- Collaborate with healthcare providers (e.g., Mayo Clinic) to tokenize legacy datasets.  
- Advocate for regulatory approval (e.g., HIPAA compliance via ZKPs).  

#### **Impact**:  
- Accelerates medical research while protecting patient privacy.  
- Patients earn BSV for contributing data.  

---

### **4. Decentralized Content Delivery**  
**Use Case**: A Netflix-like platform where users pay **per second of video watched**, with revenue split between creators and CDNs.  

#### **Technical Steps**:  
- **Microtransaction Streaming**:  
  - Integrate BSV wallets into video players for real-time nanopayments (e.g., $0.0001 per second).  
- **Content Tokenization**:  
  - Mint tokens for movies/shows, enabling resale or fractional ownership.  
....
(Continues in next post)

title: **Story Protocol** is a blockchain-based platform designed to revolutionize how creators manage, monetize, and collaborate on intellectual property (IP). By leveraging blockchain technology, it solves
content:
**Story Protocol** is a blockchain-based platform designed to revolutionize how creators manage, monetize, and collaborate on intellectual property (IP). By leveraging blockchain technology, it solves critical challenges like attribution, licensing, and royalty distribution. Below is a detailed **use case explanation** with a practical example to illustrate its value:

---

### **Problem Statement: Traditional IP Management**
Creators (writers, artists, filmmakers) face significant hurdles when managing their IP:
1. **Attribution**: Difficulty proving ownership or tracking unauthorized use.
2. **Licensing**: Manual, opaque processes for granting rights to adaptations (e.g., fan fiction, films).
3. **Royalty Distribution**: Complex revenue-sharing for derivative works (e.g., sequels, translations).
4. **Collaboration**: No transparent way to co-create while ensuring fair compensation.

---

### **How Story Protocol Solves This**
Story Protocol uses blockchain to:
- **Mint IP as programmable assets**: Stories, characters, or universes are registered on-chain.
- **Track lineage**: Every adaptation or derivative work is recorded in an "IP graph."
- **Automate payments**: Smart contracts split royalties in real time based on predefined terms.

---

### **Example Use Case: Collaborative Storytelling & Monetization**
**Scenario**:  
*Alice*, a sci-fi writer, publishes a short story called *"Galactic Orphans"* on Story Protocol. She wants to allow others to build on her work while ensuring she earns royalties from derivatives.

#### **Step 1: Minting the Original IP**
- Alice uploads *"Galactic Orphans"* to Story Protocol and **mints it as an IP asset**.
- The blockchain records her ownership and sets **licensing terms** (e.g., 10% royalty on all derivatives).

#### **Step 2: Derivative Work Creation**
- *Bob*, a fan fiction writer, writes a sequel: *"Galactic Orphans: The Exile."*  
  - Bob licenses Alice’s story via Story Protocol’s smart contract.  
  - The platform automatically links Bob’s sequel to Alice’s original work in the **IP graph**.  
- *Carol*, an artist, creates a comic adaptation of Alice’s story.  
  - Carol pays a one-time licensing fee (handled by smart contract) and agrees to share 5% of her comic’s revenue with Alice.

#### **Step 3: Automated Royalty Distribution**
- When Bob’s sequel earns $1,000 from ebook sales:  
  - **10% ($100)** is automatically sent to Alice’s wallet.  
  - Bob keeps the remaining $900.  
- When Carol’s comic earns $500:  
  - **5% ($25)** goes to Alice.  
  - Carol keeps $475.  

#### **Step 4: Further Derivatives**
- A game studio, *Stellar Games*, licenses Bob’s sequel to create a video game.  
  - Royalties flow to **both Alice and Bob** based on the IP graph:  
    - Alice earns 10% of Bob’s original 10% cut.  
    - Bob earns 90% of the 10% cut.  

---

### **Key Benefits**
1. **Transparency**: Every adaptation is tracked on-chain, eliminating disputes over ownership.  
2. **Automation**: Royalties are paid instantly via smart contracts, reducing administrative overhead.  
3. **Collaboration**: Creators can safely invite others to build on their work, expanding their story’s universe.  
4. **New Revenue Streams**: Even small contributions (e.g., fan art, translations) generate income.  

---

### **Real-World Analogy**
Imagine **Marvel Comics** using Story Protocol:  
- Stan Lee’s original *Spider-Man* would be minted as an IP asset.  
- Every movie, toy, or fan comic would automatically pay royalties to Marvel (and Lee’s estate) via smart contracts.  
- Contributors (writers, artists) could earn a share of revenue from future adaptations.  

---

### **Future Use Cases**
1. **AI-Generated Content**:  
   - If an AI model trains on Alice’s story to generate new content, Story Protocol could enforce royalties for Alice.  
2. **Global Collaboration**:  
   - A writer in Nigeria and an animator in South Korea could co-cr story, with revenue split fairly.

title:
ALPHABET/GOOGLE $GOOGL WALL ST. POST-EARNINGS COMMENTARY

Truist Securities - Buy, $225 PT

"We remain constructive on GOOGL following a broad-based beat across Search, Cloud and Devices with improvin
content:
ALPHABET/GOOGLE $GOOGL WALL ST. POST-EARNINGS COMMENTARY

Truist Securities - Buy, $225 PT

"We remain constructive on GOOGL following a broad-based beat across Search, Cloud and Devices with improving margins, even as mgmt continues to invest against its highest priorities, AI, Tech infra and YT. While still early to properly gauge the ROI on these investments, we're seeing green shoots in Search with higher engagement and introduction of ads within AIO, and in Cloud with Y/Y growth acceleration driven by AI Infra and GenAI. GOOGL remains at the forefront of the AI race, which is likely to expand the TAM for Search and Cloud, fuel growth higher for longer and drive shareholder value. We adjust our ests and PT to $225 from $220."

Bank of America - Buy, $210 PT

"3Q net revenue at $74.5bn beat Street at $72.8bn driven by Cloud and other revs. Search revenues grew 12.2% y/y (roughly in line), while YouTube grew 12% y/y (vs 11%), both decelerating q/q. A big Cloud beat was a positive surprise with growth accelerating 6pt q/q to 35% y/y (vs Street 29%) and margins were up 582bps q/q to 17% (AI cycle for cloud a positive read for AWS). Other income was $3.2bn (vs BofA ests. for $0.8bn), driving $0.16 of the EPS beat. EPS was $2.12, well above Street at $1.85. Another quarter of solid results suggest that company-wide Al benefits are outweighing any disruption headwinds and Alphabet is net Al beneficiary (Cloud in 3Q, Search in 2Q). We acknowledge potential negative catalysts (Meta AI and ChatGPT product rollouts, DoJ filing), but think Street could be overlooking AI Overview benefits next year. Also, with headcount flattish y/y, we continue to think Alphabet has a positive cost basis set up to drive strong EPS growth. We are raising our PO slightly to $210 (from $206) based on higher 2025 core EPS and unchanged 22x P/E multiple, plus cash. At AH price of $181, we est. Google core Search business trades at 15x 2025 EPS, attractive vs S&P at 21x."

JPMorgan - Overweight, $212 PT

"Key standouts for us coming out of 3Q earnings were: 1) Google’s increased confidence in its AI Search rollout; 2) strong Google Cloud acceleration (+35% Y/Y) & margin expansion (17.1% OPM); & 3) operating profit upside (32.3% OPM) w/continued commitment to re-engineer the cost structure under new CFO Anat Ashkenazi. Google reiterated that AI Overviews are increasing overall Search engagement & user satisfaction, & also monetizing at roughly the same rate as non-AI searches. More broadly Google emphasized it is well-positioned to lead in AI through its full-stack approach including robust AI infrastructure, strong research & model capabilities, and product & platform reach. GOOGL’s FXHN revenue growth accelerated in 3Q to 16.4% led by upside in Cloud and Subscriptions, Platforms, & Devices (SPD), while Search & YouTube were more in line w/expectations. Investor pushback will likely come from lack of upside in Search — which decelerated to ~13.5% Y/Y FXHN but also faced a tougher comp — and YouTube, which saw similar dynamics but also benefited from election-related ad tailwind in 3Q. Attention will also turn to final proposed DOJ remedies in the search commercial agreements trial due November 20. A frequent discussion w/investors into earnings was whether Google could post a quarter that would shift the narrative from the search trial w/the DOJ & enable the stock to break out — we think this one comes pretty close. We remain positive on GOOGL shares & raise our December 2025 PT to $212 based on ~20x our ‘26E GAAP EPS of $10.72."

Goldman Sachs - Buy, $208 PT

"We expect the market to have a positive reaction to Alphabet’s Q3'24 earnings report upon our first read/analysis of the results. In terms of upside, we would point to Search & Other revenue (which exceeded our forecasts at +12.2% YoY growth), YouTube Ads revenue growing +12.2% YoY (slightly above our forecasts and some investor fears of a more pronounced deceleration from Q2 levels) & Google Cloud revenue beating our estimates by ~5% and growing +35% YoY, demonstrating very strong reacceleration. In addition, both Google Services Operating Income (~7% ahead of our estimate) and Google Cloud Operating Income (~68% above our estimate) far exceeded expectations as Alphabet continued to demonstrate a balance of efficiencies and long-term investments in its core business and continued incremental margin progression in its Cloud segment. In terms of the upcoming earnings call, we would expect the focus to be on the broader digital advertising trends (end demand, shortened holiday period, long/short form video consumption/monetization trends & the broad implementation of AI across an array of advertising platforms and products), the environment for Cloud in terms of broader adoption trends, AI tailwinds and workload migration and how Alphabet’s new CFO might frame narratives around key priorities (especially around a balance between efficiencies and long-term investments)."

Barclays - Overweight, $220 PT

"The Key Take-Away: GOOGL reported revenue and EPS that were 2% and 15% above consensus. Search and YouTube were broadly in line, Cloud handily beat expectations, and operating margins were flat and well above what the street was modeling. The new CFO had a smooth transition, and while the investment community is clamoring for more details on various aspects of Alphabet’s business, given the legal drama surrounding GOOGL, we weren’t surprised to hear the same high-level communication philosophy carried out. We shift back into regulatory headline risk in a few weeks with Google’s suggested remedy package followed by the DoJ’s detailed asks, so we think upside for GOOGL shares near-term is somewhat capped. The 20x EPS multiple reflects some of the regulatory and AI related risks, which we continue to muddle through ever so slowly."

Piper Sandler - Overweight, $210 PT

"Google reported a solid 3Q with mid-teens revenue growth and revenue and op income 2% and 8% above our estimates. Gemini could be a profound tailwind to the ad stack and AI overlays are expanding and taking hold. Results at Cloud and within Youtube subscriptions stood out. A nice debut for new CFO Ashkenazi, the op margin result was a positive surprise. We raise estimates post quarter, price target to $210, reiterate OW."

Evercore - Outperform, $205 PT

"Our View: We reiterate our Outperform on GOOGL in the wake of a Solid Beat Q3 print, and modestly raise our PT to $205 from $200 prior. Q3 Revenue performance came in above the Street across segments and Op Margin was solidly higher than expectations. While we remain concerned by risks related to the DOJ Search trial, we think GOOGL is making a compelling case for the company’s continued leadership in the AI era, helping to address one key element of the bear case around the company’s “terminal” value. With this note, we are closing our TAP Outperform call."
author: stocktalkweekly

title:
For people that dont want to watch the video to hear my critique... (long post incoming!)
Why is BS🦋 not that great of an alternative?

0. For the two reasons that people left, 🦋 is a worse alternativ
content:
For people that dont want to watch the video to hear my critique... (long post incoming!)
Why is BS🦋 not that great of an alternative?

0. For the two reasons that people left, 🦋 is a worse alternative.
- Blocks were always easily circumventable with alt accounts and 🦋 can NOT protect your posts from third party tools.
- The danger of AI is even more prevalent on 🦋, due to the lack of third party protection in addition that your posts are not even hidden behind a login-wall.
There is a lot of misinformation and delusions surrounding this topic atm and switching platforms because of that is irrational.
(More about AI in the near future)

1. Bluesky is Open Source AND decentralized.
Meaning the code is publicly avaialble and there is no company behind this site or a corresponding structure that could support it properly.
That leads to a VERY slow development and bad actors easier time to adjust things like bots to their needs. And as far as i know there is also no detection system in place to counter that.

2. A Beta on a very slow trail
The site currently is pretty much like an early beta of its big brother, with a load of missing basic features, such as Drafts, Scheduled Posts, Bookmarks, a Thread function or collapsable Replies. And due to the slow development, it cant be expected to catch up in reasonable time.
Im on this platform for about a year and barely anything noticeable changed since then.
To make a point how lacking it is... If people switched two months ago, they wouldnt even be able to upload videos to it.

3. Moderation is basically impossible or arbitrary
Due to the lack of a serious structure, we have to ask WHO gets our reports and on what basis they make decisions. Nobody wants Reddit or Discord Mods to decide about right or wrong.
And even on sites like Twitch, where we can see for the past months, that even within companies where people could technically be held accountable, it still happens that people abuse their power to target certain groups (e.g. Vtubers) or apply their TOS very unequally (see the recent antisemitism situation).

4. The "New Platform effect"
Seeing people say how great BS🦋 is, because they get the same interactions with just 10% of the following, seemingly dont think that really through.
Why is that the case? Well... if you look at many peoples profiles on TwiXer, they are following hundreds, if not THOUSANDS of people. And the same is true for their followers. Now guess what happens when they move to a new platform and are suddenly only following a small percentage of the accounts, that an algorithm previously somehow had to filter through, to recommend posts from?
The attention is more focused on the smaller userbase and the smaller amount of people they are following, focusing the activity at the smaller selection of posts they can see.
- The Problem -
People dont change. And the same people that are following 2000 people here, will gather the same amount there over time.
The same people that are incapable of moderating and cleaning up their timelines or flock to every drama possible, will drift into the same spheres on 🦋.
And we will have the same issues there over time, that we have here. But all that within systems that can not support or compensate for that. (the things ive mentioned earlier)
So people would have to use TwiXer-freak features like the block-lists and we all know how well that usually works. With the lack of wider algorithms, we might also have the big issue that we will get very homogenous echo chambers (look at Reddit how well that often goes). Tho if thats a pro or con for you, is up to your imagination, but i personally appreciate seeing different perspectives on topics.

5. Pseudo-verification.
The site pretends like the "custom domain handles" are some form of a verification. While they are actually making impersonation WAY easier.
The problem is - custom domains are cheap and can use the same name.
Meaning, instead of the default (callmeteci .bsky .social) handle, i could change my handle into:
callmeteci .com or .social or .live or .org and so on.
So if someone does not know what specific domain ending you have, it is very easy for them to impersonate you on first glance or make themselves look more trustworthy than they are. Scammers like that a lot.

6. Lack of monetization
No ads or monetization might sound great for you, but a company with no proper income, but 13M registered accounts will get into trouble sooner or later. Especially after they lost the support of Ex Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey earlier this year.
Just today they announced that they fundraised $15M. The lead money-giver among them: Blockchain Capital.🫠
And they announced a Premium sub "for higher video quality and greater customizability of the profile page".
I can already tell you, a Premium Sub will NOT work to offset the running costs of the website, so we will have to see for how long they can pretend to be holier than the pope and not implement ads or some crypto-bs, what would be even worse imo.
Problem with ads is also that (to my knowledge) there is no system in place to distribute them properly among the userbase. That significantly reduces the value that advertisers can get out of it, leading to less income from them. And then we circle back again to the slow development of the site, due to the lack of resources, manpower or structure... and i hope you slowly get the picture here. 🫠

They COULD change into a project that would work like the Open Source Project "Blender", what is very similarly manged like a real company would, but considering that Blender is pretty much a unicorn in the Open Source space, that is very unlikely to happen. (And kinda explains why most Open Source projects s'ck in comparison to the products from companies)
More about that here: youtube .com/watch?v=nHQv4blla7g

And all that leads me to the simple conclusion that Bluesky will become not just the same, but significantly worse over time than its big brother ever could. (yeah, sounds crazy, i know)
I didnt saw attempts to turn things into a significantly more efficient direction in the past year (even with Dorsey on board) and therefore i dont expect that to change in the near future either.

And how it currently is, i feel the major influx of users over the past few months was more a curse for the project than anything else.
We need to keep in mind that BS🦋 was an experiment from Twitter for the tech behind it (AT protocol) and never meant to be an actual Twitter competitor in the foreseeable future.
Tho i also never understood why we need a second decentralized social network protocol, after the Fediverse around Mastodon already has tech that pretty much does the same. But hey nobody asked for ten dozen Firefox forks or buckets worth of Linux OS' either, so i guess thats just an Open Source thing. 🤷

And with all that said... why does nobody migrate to Tumblr instead? ;D

Thanks for the read and have a nice day!
author: CallMeTeci

title:
$UMG - Pershing Square current position and remains positive on the stock.

Thesis - Music growth remains positive, room to inc prices, competent mgmt, current valuation much lower than intrinsic valu
content:
$UMG - Pershing Square current position and remains positive on the stock.

Thesis - Music growth remains positive, room to inc prices, competent mgmt, current valuation much lower than intrinsic value

"Universal Music Group UMG’s shares experienced a sharp drop when the company reported results last month. While the company’s overall revenue growth of 10% and operating income growth of 11% were both strong, subscription and streaming revenue growth, a key metric, decelerated during the quarter from its recent double-digit growth rate to mid-single digit growth. We believe the quarter’s disappointing subscription and streaming growth is due to certain idiosyncratic factors unique to UMG combined with some weakening in the overall economic environment. As evidenced by UMG’s peers’ results however, music streaming is still growing at a healthy rate. We believe that UMG’s underperformance this quarter will prove to be shortterm in nature and does not impact our view of UMG’s medium and long-term growth prospects. We continue to believe that music has a long runway of future growth, as it remains under-monetized relative to history and when compared to other forms of media. We expect the industry to improve monetization through new products and services, with better segmentation of customers including higher-priced tiers and increased subscription prices. UMG’s subscription revenue growth of 7% slowed from 13% last quarter, as the company began to lap last year’s price increases. Slower growth at certain digital service providers (“DSPs”) offset strong growth at Spotify and YouTube. While quarterly performance can fluctuate, we believe that each of UMG’s core DSPs has a healthy business and that UMG can further drive streaming and subscription growth by working with its DSP partners to improve their offerings. The company is now working with Spotify in launching a premium offering for superfans which UMG estimates could ultimately be adopted by as much as 20% of Spotify’s subscriber base. We believe there is ample room to increase pricing in the coming years as music subscriptions have been kept at flat prices for nearly a decade until some recent increases. As the industry matures in developed markets, ad-supported users who today receive free music can be charged a monthly subscription fee, as is typically the case in the video streaming industry. While each of the major DSPs increased prices for individual subscriptions from $9.99 to $10.99, only Spotify and Deezer have raised prices to $11.99 and only in certain geographies and for certain plans. During the quarter, UMG’s streaming revenues (revenues from ad-supported music) declined by 4%, a sharp deceleration from double-digit growth last quarter, as economic uncertainty caused a slowdown in advertising revenues from its largest partners. The decline was also caused by the absence of revenues from TikTok while a new deal was being negotiated which did not start until the beginning of May. UMG’s revenues from Meta also decreased temporarily while the companies worked together on a more holistic deal that will grow other aspects of their relationship. While streaming revenues are less predictable because they are much more susceptible to economic conditions, we believe that over the long term they should grow at a similar or higher rate than subscription revenue growth. UMG’s management team led by Sir Lucian Grainge has a long track-record of growing and shaping the music market by working with its partners in innovating creative solutions to drive growth. For example, UMG’s efforts led to the industry-wide adoption of “artist-centric” initiatives that will result in a greater share of streaming royalties for its artists. UMG is also leading the industry by working with partners to launch new products to harness AI’s growth opportunities while also ensuring regulatory and legal protection for its artists. Similar to how investors initially overreacted to concerns about the potential negative impact from AI, only to see UMG shares quickly recover as the market better understood the AI risk, we believe that as investors better understand UMG’s path to higher revenue growth and regain confidence in the long-term health of the industry, the company’s share price is likely to increase significantly from its current levels. To that end, the company is hosting a Capital Markets Day in September which is the ideal forum for management to provide investors with more details on its business and the long-term growth opportunity. Given UMG’s strong market position and long runway for sustained earnings growth, we believe that the company’s current valuation represents a deep discount to its intrinsic value."
author: stockoholics

title:
META reported a solid qtr.

Revs of 36.5b were up 23% yy cc against an easy comp (+3% last Q2).  Worth noting US / CAN only grew 11% yy.  Operating income of 14.8b (41% margin) up 58% yy.  Guided to 3
content:
META reported a solid qtr.

Revs of 36.5b were up 23% yy cc against an easy comp (+3% last Q2).  Worth noting US / CAN only grew 11% yy.  Operating income of 14.8b (41% margin) up 58% yy.  Guided to 38.5-41.0b in revs up 24% yy against a +11% comp last Q3.

No real change from last qtr re: commentary on engagement / monetization drivers, opex discipline, capex investment in AI.

Seems like $25 is a reasonable EPS bogey to anchor on for 2025.  Every 5b of additional opex is $2 op inc hit or $1.60 EPS hit assuming 20% tax rate.  So even assuming 9b of incremental investment goes to 16b next year would imply EPS of ~$23 all else being equal which is also where street is today.  Hard to see that being worth less than 18x outside a recession which is $414 which is -18% downside.  

Base case of 25x $25 = $625 or 25% up from here.  Add another 15-20% upside into 2026.  And serious further upside if they nail the AI agent opportunity.  Also EPS would be 25-30% higher along w/ the stock if mixed reality ever stops burning mountains of cash.

Company is doing just fine so I’ll only highlight caution points / questions:
-Ad impression growth of 10% is really slowing across the platform, why? [They said mix of video to shorts will increase opportunity for ads]

-Said US young adult user growth is good but then sounded like maybe this is just Groups / Marketplace [Fine, but would rather they flag growth in core FB / IG]
-Said positive on WhatsApp usage in US [really? I don’t know anyone who uses it domestically for chat, and I use it to chat internationally]

-Most GenAI use cases seem incremental today – continuation of targeting improvement from machine learning, ad image editing, etc.

-Newer GenAI use cases seem very early [role playing services? Ummmm.]  Said they would have top assistant by year-end but I’m not sure what they have to back that up given their current AI search is the same as all the others.  Worth noting they also think their VR is better than AAPL VR so could just be biased [I have no opinion on whose is better]

-GenAI agents could be in business chats and drive messaging revenue.  [This seems plausible if the tech works.]
-Next version of Llama will take 10x the compute to train. [although aren’t NVDA chips increasing in productivity exponentially as well?  Blackwell is 2.5x perf / chip compared to predecessor and 5 times perf for FP4]  Example: https://t.co/LIXu7I8kWX

Notes

Users
-DAU up 7% yy
-WhatsApp in US now has 100m MAU
-Seeing good growth in young adults in US on platform – sounds like Groups / Marketplaces [yawn]
-Threats hit 200m MAU – seeing deeper engagement
-More than 50% of posts come from original posts
-Unified video player being rolled out in app
-More short form videos in Reels will increase ad opportunities

Monetization
-Impressions up 10% yy w/ pricing up 10% yy
-Room to increase ads on video
-Advantage+ campaign tools give advertisers 22% higher return per study

AI
-Drives recommendations / ad targeting on platform – increasing engagement on Reels more than CPUs did
-Creative content creation for advertisers over time will be important – GenAI tools for image expansion, background / text generation used by 1m advertisers
-New AI services: role-play conversations, search, imagine yourself [sound like features]
-AI Studio so people can create agents – chat w/ their community, answer questions, etc. [not sure I understand use case here]
-On track to be most used assistant in the world by end of year [what?]
-Business AI – alpha testing for now and vision is every business has an agent in addition to website, social, etc.  Think will dramatically increase messaging rev.
-Ross Sandler said META trained Llama 3.1 on 16k H100s and META would have 600k avail by year-end

Llama
-Pushing open-source bc want to be involved w/ leading model and leverage external development
-Will need 10x compute to train Llama 4 [although aren’t NVDA chips increasing in productivity exponentially as well?  Blackwell is 2.5x perf / chip compared to predecessor and 5 times perf for FP4]  Example: https://t.co/LIXu7I8kWX

VR/AR
-Ahead of expectations [but only grew rev 28% yy while burn increased from 3.8b last qtr to 4.5b this qtr?  Like adoption here is not commensurate w/ investment at all]

Qtr
-Rev up 23% yy cc
-Headcount down 1% yy
-CFO of 19.4b up 12% yy
-CAPEX of 8.2b up 34% yy
-Reality Labs burned 4.5b in the qtr up from 3.8b last qtr – basically profits would be 33% higher if not for this

Guidance
-Growth moderates in Q3 due to lapping ramp of China-based advertisers and strong Reels impression growth
-Reality labs losses to increase meaningfully this year
-2024 CAPEX of 37-40b
-Infra costs will be significant driver of expense growth next year – D&A / opex
author: TechFundies

title:
The Sip Report #002: 📵 Build a digital addiction management platform

💡 The Big Idea
Develop a comprehensive digital addiction management platform that combines educational content, behaviour tracking
content:
The Sip Report #002: 📵 Build a digital addiction management platform

💡 The Big Idea
Develop a comprehensive digital addiction management platform that combines educational content, behaviour tracking, and community support to help individuals recognise and manage their digital dependence.

The Sip Report breaks down startup ideas from @gregisenberg & his guests on the wildly popular business pod The Startup Ideas Podcast, with deep-dive research into the opportunity for aspiring entrepreneurs & innovation junkies. 

Reference episode: https://t.co/bezsl8i3iP
Guest: @Jicecream 🙌

Let's dig into it. 

🧩The Problem

Digital dependence. Screen time has become part of the fabric of everyday life.

In today's world, and especially post-COVID, excessive use of smartphones and digital devices is pervasive, yet many of us are not fully aware of the detrimental impact it can have on our lives. 

Or we might be aware, but choose to to ignore it, due to the convenience and gratification the devices provide. 

All this time spent online can lead to: 
• poor concentration
• sleep issues
• poor mental health
• anxiety
• unhealthy eating habits
• self-esteem issues
• lack of physical activity
• gaining weight
• social withdrawal

The problem in numbers: 

• Globally, people average 6h 58m of screen time per day
• South Africans spend 10h 46m on screen per day
• Almost half of 0 to 2-year-olds interact with smartphones
• Gen Z averages around 9 hours of screen time per day
• American teenagers from lower-income households  spend 9h 19m on their screens each day
• 60% of children in the UK exhibit concerning behaviours
• The average person does 650 device unlocks every day

💊 The Value Proposition

• Education. Providing users with in-depth content and coaching on the effects of digital addiction and strategies to mitigate it, similar to the educational approach used by health apps like @zerolongevity 

• Behaviour tracking. Tools, apps and/or wearables to monitor and analyze screen time and app usage, helping users understand their digital habits and set goals to reduce usage.

• Community support. Create IRL meet-ups where users can share experiences, find support, and participate in workshops or training sessions led by experts. 

💪 Killer Features

• Interactive courses. Engaging and informative courses that guide users through understanding and managing digital addiction.

• Voice-based AI coach. Whilst digital, voice-based chat (like with ChatGPT) doesn't involve screens. 

The soon-to-be-released version from OpenAI is very human-like, so this form factor can help drive a shift from screen to voice.

It's curbing screen time, avoids doomscrolling urges,  and limits distractions. A move in the right direction.

• Usage analytics. Detailed reports and insights into users' screen time and app usage patterns.

• Goal setting and streaks. Features to help users set reduction goals and maintain streaks to encourage positive digital habits.

• Expert content. Articles, videos, and live sessions from digital wellness experts.

⏰ Why Now?

Signs of a rising concern about digital wellness. There's more & more coverage of the adverse effects of excessive screen time. Books like Digital Minimalism @Calnewport_ are gaining big followings.

The rise of dumb phones: some analysts predict dumb phone sales could grow to 5% of the entire US handset market over the next several years. 

These are phones with purely basic functions such as calling, texting and setting alarms.  

The Minimal Phone from @minimalcompany (on pre-order) uses e-ink to reduce eye strain, has a physical keyboard, and provides a curated suite of productivity apps without the bloat.

Even teenagers are starting to embrace a smartphone liberation movement. Known as "neo-Luddites", there's an expanding group who are going cold turkey. 

You'd have first mover advantage. While there are tools that help limit device usage, a comprehensive platform that combines education, tracking, and community support is unique in the market.

🍕 Market Size

• Total Addressable Market: the global wellness market is projected to reach $4.24 trillion by 2026​​. 

Within this, the digital wellness segment is estimated to reach $1.1 trillion by 2028.

• Serviceable Addressable Market: let's assume the portion of the digital wellness market that is specifically focused on addressing digital addiction and excessive screen time is 10 - 15%, or $137.5bn. 

• Serviceable Obtainable Market: For a new product in this space, a reasonable initial SOM could be around 1% of the SAM - $1.375bn

🌱 Early Adopters

• Millennials and Gen Z have grown up with digital devices and are often heavy users of smartphones, social media, and other digital platforms. 

• Urban populations tend to have higher exposure to digital technologies and may be more susceptible to digital addiction. 

• Affluent individuals and households with higher disposable incomes are more likely to afford and adopt digital devices and services, potentially increasing their risk of digital addiction.

For our initial "super fans" we could niche down to startup founders. Because they:

• have extreme focus and productivity needs

• experience high levels of personal/professional overlap

• are usually on the leading edge of tech adoption & appreciate well-designed products that provide genuine utility

• are influential voices: even micro-influencers in the startup community wield outsized influence. Winning over just a few respected startup voices could catalyze viral adoption among their peers.

• have a higher willingness to pay - bootstrapped startups invest carefully, but founders place high value on tools that provide a competitive edge. 

🥊 Competition

Focus apps like 

• Forest - https://t.co/hGg3EFG7E7 - plant a tree when you want to focus, finish early and it dies

• @freedom - block apps and websites on every device

• Timers and tools like @focuskeeper that use the Pomodoro technique to boost productivity.

• @one_sec_app - a plugin that makes users take a deep breath before they can open and access the apps on their phone

• @opalapp - see your screen time and focus change in real time; claims you will reclaim 6 years of your life

Counterpunch: Position the platform as an all-in-one solution with unique educational content and community / support network.

Parental control & screen time management tools

• Built-in parental controls on devices and operating systems (iOS Screen Time, Google Family Link)

• Third-party parental control apps like @qustodio and @OurPactApp 

• Screen time monitoring apps like @screentimelabs 

Counterpunch: Position as a solution for individual self-improvement and personal growth, rather than just a parental control tool.

Professional coaches and therapists specializing in digital wellness and addiction recovery

• Online coaching platforms and apps like @betterhelp  and @talkspace 

Counterpunch: Offer a more affordable and accessible alternative to traditional coaching and therapy through the AI-powered coaching experience. Make it a blended approach, combining digital tools with human expert support and community groups.

🍌 Risks

Convincing users to change deeply ingrained digital habits and break addictive patterns can be extremely difficult.

Mitigation: use proven behavior change techniques, incentives, gamification, and leverage community support.

Major tech companies and social media platforms may view the platform as a threat to their user engagement and monetization strategies.

Mitigation: develop platform-agnostic solutions, foster strategic partnerships.

The "screen positivists" could become noisier, especially if governments attempt to step in with regulation. 

• A 2022 study found little association between recreational screen time and children's cognitive abilities when factoring in sleep, exercise and gameplay.

• Anthropologists point out humans have always been "addicted" to new technologies and media throughout history (novels, TV, video games).

• Some experts believe quality family time and overall lifestyle factors matter more than arbitrary screen time limits.

Mitigation: instead of taking an absolutist anti-screen time stance, position the platform as promoting balanced, intentional, self-regulated tech use that aligns with each individual's needs and goals.

🧪 MVP

• Create a course specifically tailored for startup founders on managing digital addiction while maintaining productivity; include real-life examples and case studies from successful entrepreneurs.

• Develop an app that not only tracks screen time but also integrates with productivity tools like Slack, Trello etc to analyze work-related vs. non-work-related usage.

• Set up a private community for startup founders to share experiences, tips, and support each other.

• Organize virtual meetups and mastermind groups for founders to discuss challenges and strategies in managing digital addiction.

• Implement a voice-based AI coach (an OpenAI wrapper is fine, perhaps using @elevenlabs for the voice element) that offers personalized digital wellness advice and motivation tailored to the unique pressures of startup founders.

• Partner with startup incubators and accelerators to pilot the platform with their cohorts. Gather detailed feedback to refine the product.

• Use pilot testers as early ambassadors to promote the platform within their networks.

💳 Monetisation

Freemium model: Offer basic features for free, with premium content and advanced tools available via subscription.

Corporate packages: Sell tailored packages to businesses for employee digital wellness programs.

Workshops and training: Offer paid IRL workshops and training sessions led by digital wellness experts.

🛣️ Roadmap

• Implement competitive challenges and badges to motivate and engage users

• Create founder-specific rewards, such as access to exclusive startup tools

• Develop AI-driven foresight tools that predict burnout and recommend preventative measures

• Create a non-profit arm to advocate for digital wellness education and policy changes globally

That's it for this report! Thanks so much for reading. Follow to get your regular glug 🙏
author: martinslaney

title:
$GOOGL blew off the barn doors with accelerating results in nearly every business line and pristine commentary.  Story is getting better and numbers moving up.

Risk / reward still interesting even at
content:
$GOOGL blew off the barn doors with accelerating results in nearly every business line and pristine commentary.  Story is getting better and numbers moving up.

Risk / reward still interesting even at afterhours price.  From $175, 30% upside to 25x my 2025 GAAP EPS estimate and 54% upside on 2026.  Downside of 19% to 18x street 2025 GAAP EPS (which will move up tomorrow).

Total rev of 80.5b grew 15% yy > street at 78.7b, and GAAP OP of 25.5b (32% margin) grew 32% yy > street at 22.4b.

Search: Rev of 46.2b accelerated to 14.4%.  They are not seeing any impact from competitors.  In fact, they are seeing increased search usage of new AI-powered results.  They are bringing AI-powered overviews into the search page – doing this in a measured fashion to improve search experience while prioritizing traffic to merchants and other sites.  Also highlighted that the costs of GenAI search have come down 80% from launch a little over a year ago.  [Serious three punch combo to bear case that 1) AI search goes to competition, 2) monetization goes from 5 links to 1, and 3) cost of GenAI search is much higher].

YouTube: Rev of 8.1b had large acceleration to 21% yy.  Highlighted that they saw strength from both DR / brand, and that short video monetization relative to regular has doubled yy and was up 10% qq.  This print is a big deal if it is a leading indicator bc YouTube business grew between 4-9b each year from 2019-2021, 400m in 2022 and 2b in 2023.  If this growth rate holds, they will grow by 6b this year so it may be growth is re-invigorated here.  A ban of Tiktok will only help.

Cloud: Rev of 9.6b accelerated to 28% yy w/ rev delta of 382m vs 139m last Q1 (which had optimizations).  They highlighted their strength in offering their own TPUs alongside NVDA, and that 65% of new GenAI startups are customers.  Operating income of 9.4% was flat qq.

They highlighted that YouTube and Cloud are going to exit the year at over 100b.  YouTube did 8b in Q1 and Cloud did 10b so 18b total or 72b annualized.  So they expect this to grow by over 39% through this year?!?  I can’t get there – am at 10.7b for YT in Q4 and 11.9b for Cloud Q4 so 90b annualized or 10% below their commentary.  They know the business better than me so I hope they are right.

Network: Rev of 7.4b shrank 1.1% yy.  They don’t really talk about what is going on here.  I assume GenAI searches hurt this business line over time (no more recipe sites filled to the gills w/ ads).

Subscriptions: Rev of 8.7b grew 18% yy down from 23% last qtr but up from 9% last Q1.  They increased price in May of last year so will need to work through that w/ likely slower growth rates for balance of year.

Other bets: Rev of 0.5b grew 72% yy.  Waymo is now accepting paying riders in LA and opening up test rides in Austin.  Other bets burned 1.0b in the qtr improved from 1.2b burn last Q1.  They said they have additional “workflows” to reduce this burn further.  I wonder how much of this is Waymo which I’m pretty excited about (the tech works and they are rolling it out).  In any case, we likely have seen peak profit headwind here unless they go into a massive Waymo investment cycle (which I think would be smart).

Costs: Company clearly focused on keeping these under control and said will expand margins this year.  Within that envelope, GM increased to 58.1% from 56.1% last year but gains likely moderate given increased D&A from the high AI-related capex.  Headcount shrank 1% qq.  Stock comp was DOWN 1% yy (6.5% of revs).

Margins: GAAP op margin of 31.6% - an all-time high.  Normalized for severance costs they were at 32.5% up from 30% last Q1 (also normalized).

Great qtr - lots of engines chugging here.
author: TechFundies

title: META put up a decent qtr (beating slightly) and guided more or less in-line but highlighted they will enter an AI investment cycle.  Seems to me story is getting better (core engines chugging along w/
content:
META put up a decent qtr (beating slightly) and guided more or less in-line but highlighted they will enter an AI investment cycle.  Seems to me story is getting better (core engines chugging along w/ potential ban of TikTok reducing competition, AI under development) while numbers likely move sideways due to AI investment (vs expectations that we were in beat / raise mode).

Revenue drivers seem intact (user base increasing, engagement increasing w/ help of AI (rolling out Reels recommendation engine to other surfaces, GenAI makes it easier for users to create more content and advertisers to create static / video content), targeting increasing w/ help of AI, WhatsApp monetization ramping.  Street estimates for 17.3% growth this year seems fine, and 12.5% for next year seems low – particularly if TikTok gets banned.

Opex / capex plan already seems burdened for AI investment.  Obviously GAAP operating margins can’t keep expanding at the same rate (25% in ’22 to 35% in ’23 to 39% this year).  But notably GAAP opex in street models already includes 9b of incremental investment this year and 11b next year.  And that still results in GAAP operating margins expanding another 100 bps next year and mid-teens EPS growth in 2025.  I guess it is hard to handicap what META means by “investment” – I would have never even guessed it was possible to spend 12b / year developing a VR headset (NASA spent 21.4b on its Manned Lunar Landing Program between 1961 and 1969).

The AI investment seems very smart to me.  They are basically one of three companies that can develop and roll-out an everyday AI assistant at global scale (AAPL / GOOGL / META) and hard to see how that doesn’t boost engagement / monetization over time.

Seems like $25 is a reasonable EPS bogey to anchor on for 2025.  Every 5b of additional opex is $2 op inc hit or $1.60 EPS hit assuming 20% tax rate.  So even assuming 9b of incremental investment goes to 16b next year would imply EPS of ~$23 all else being equal which is also where street is today.  Hard to see that being worth less than 18x outside a recession which is $414 – I mean it’s not like EPS is going to go backwards (right?).  Base case of 25x $25 = $625 or 50% up from here.  And serious further upside if they nail the AI agent opportunity.

Other notable highlights
-Impressions up 20% yy
-ARPU up 6% yy; advertisers that use Advantage Plus saw 28% decrease in CPC
-Think search is fundamentally different from what META aims to offer
-Seem focused on agent opportunity – cost of messaging is high today [I’m assuming they mean cost of having someone respond / send out messages]

Notes

Metrics highlights
-Daily actives of 3.24b up 7% yy
-Impressions up 20% yy w/ ARPU up 6% yy.  Impression growth consistent in all geos but Europe which decelerated to 12% yy against an easy comp.
-Rev of 36.5b up 27% yy cc inline w/ street at 36.2b.  US / CAN growth of 21% accelerated a touch.
-Operating income of 13.8b or 38% margin (of course down from seasonal strength in 41% in Q4 but also down from 40% in Q3) – a bit ahead of street at 13.3b.  --Core operating income (ex VR) of 17.7b or 50% operating margins (up from 40% last Q1).
-Rev / user down 9% qq better than last year’s -11% seasonality
-Tax rate of 13% unusually low this qtr.

Guided to 36.5-39.0b w/ 1% FX hw .  Opex for full year guided higher to 96-99b up from 94-99b due to higher infra and legal costs.  Reality labs losses to increase meaningfully yy.  Capex of 35-40b up from prior range of 30-37b bc of AI investment.  Will also increase next year.  Tax rate of mid-teens.

Feed
-30% of posts delivered by AI rec system up 2x over last couple years
->50% of content people see on Insta is AI recommended
-Video is >60% of time spent
-Started to use AI to power Reels and saw 8-10% increase in watch time

Targeting
-Advantage plus audience targeting saw 28% decrease in CPC

Search
-Think that is a different product than what META is aiming to provide

WhatsApp
-Think cost to business of engaging in messaging is very high [assume he means people cost] so bots can really help here

AI
-See opportunity here and going to lean into investment
-Vision is to use across platform but also to develop agents
